steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence opens with an introductory participial phrase: ‘Having 11,486 feet of prominence,’ followed by a comma. The subject of the independent clause that follows must be the thing that logically ‘has’ the prominence — Mount Hayes — and it must appear immediately after the comma to avoid a dangling modifier.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — geographers have ranked Mount Hayes, a peak in the United States, as the world's 51st most prominent mountain.: The subject is ‘geographers.’ Geographers do not have 11,486 feet of prominence — dangling modifier. ✗
  • Choice B — Mount Hayes, a peak located in the United States, is ranked by geographers as the world's 51st most prominent mountain.: Mount Hayes is the subject immediately after the comma. Mount Hayes is the entity with 11,486 feet of prominence — the participial phrase modifies it correctly. ✓
  • Choice C — the list of mountains geographers rank as the world's most prominent includes Mount Hayes, a peak located in the United States, at number 51.: The subject is ‘the list.’ A list does not have topographical prominence — dangling modifier. ✗
  • Choice D — the ranking given by geographers to Mount Hayes, a peak located in the United States, is 51st most prominent mountain in the world.: The subject is ‘the ranking.’ A ranking does not have 11,486 feet of prominence — dangling modifier. ✗
That is the correct answer.
